name: Burgomillodo Dam and Cantalejo Lagoons Loop
name_original: La Presa del Pantano de Burgomillodo desde San Miguel de Bernuy — circular por las Lagunas de Cantalejo
description: This moderate 32.3-mile road cycling route offers reservoir views and passes through the unique Lagunas de Cantalejo wetlands.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Miguel De Bernuy are set within the province of Segovia, characterized by the dramatic gorges of the Duratón River and impressive limestone cliffs. The terrain offers varied elevation, with both climbs and descents, providing an engaging experience for road cyclists. Many routes feature mostly paved surfaces, suitable for various skill levels, and often parallel the river's course. This region provides a blend of natural grandeur and a network of roads for diverse cycling proficiencies. Best road cycling routes around San Miguel De Bernuy The most popular road cycling route is Burgomillodo Dam and Cantalejo Lagoons Loop, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route offers reservoir views and passes through unique wetlands. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillos de Peñafiel y Fuentidueña, por el Río Duratón — circular desde San Miguel de Bernuy, a moderate 55.6 miles (89.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and winds along the Duratón River. Local road cyclists also love the Towns of Tierra de Cuéllar — circular from San Miguel de Bernuy, a 42.3 miles (68.1 km) trail leading through the varied landscapes of Tierra de Cuéllar, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around San Miguel De Bernuy is defined by the Duratón River gorges, limestone cliffs, and varied elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from less strenuous rides to routes requiring good fitness. The routes in San Miguel De Bernuy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Miguel De Bernuy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around San Miguel De Bernuy?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around San Miguel De Bernuy, offering a variety of distances and difficulties. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes here?
A: The region is characterized by the dramatic gorges of the Duratón River and impressive limestone cliffs, providing varied elevation with both climbs and descents. Routes often wind along the river, offering breathtaking views. Many routes feature mostly paved surfaces, suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: Yes, while the area offers challenging climbs, many routes around San Miguel de Bernuy feature mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels. There are options for less strenuous rides, some lasting up to two hours with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What options are there for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced cyclists can find routes requiring good to very good fitness, some potentially including unpaved segments for an added challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069145443/castillos-de-penafiel-y-fuentiduena-por-el-rio-duraton-circular-desde-san-miguel-de-bernuy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Peñafiel y Fuentidueña, por el Río Duratón — circular desde San Miguel de Bernuy&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 55.6 miles (89.5 km) path with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural sights and viewpoints can I enjoy while road cycling?
A: You'll experience the stunning Duratón River Gorges, with routes often paralleling the river's course and offering views of its meanders and towering cliffs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353034306/burgomillodo-dam-and-cantalejo-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgomillodo Dam and Cantalejo Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers reservoir views and passes through unique wetlands. Keep an eye out for griffon vultures soaring overhead, especially from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5791277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of vulture nests&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions along the routes?
A: Yes, cycling routes are often interspersed with historical attractions. You might encounter ruins of hermitages, such as 'las ermitonas' and 'los sanpedros y los sanmartines'. The historic center of Sepúlveda is also a point of interest for longer loops. Other notable sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6204193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6204307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tombs of the Saints&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5790017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and village of Fuentidueña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Miguel De Bernuy?
A: The region offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, allowing you to fully appreciate the natural beauty of the Duratón River gorges and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around San Miguel De Bernuy are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353034306/burgomillodo-dam-and-cantalejo-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgomillodo Dam and Cantalejo Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069156372/towns-of-tierra-de-cuellar-circular-from-san-miguel-de-bernuy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towns of Tierra de Cuéllar — circular from San Miguel de Bernuy&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I observe wildlife while cycling in this region?
A: Absolutely. The Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park is home to a significant colony of griffon vultures, which you can often see soaring above the cliffs. Other wildlife like alimoches, red-billed choughs, rabbits, hares, and foxes can also be spotted, particularly when cycling quietly through the páramo areas.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in San Miguel De Bernuy?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the blend of natural grandeur, the dramatic gorges of the Duratón River, and the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, all within a culturally rich environment.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not detailed, the presence of less strenuous rides of up to two hours with minimal elevation gain suggests options suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed pace. These routes often feature mostly paved surfaces, contributing to a smoother experience.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for road cycling routes here?
A: Route distances vary significantly, from shorter rides to longer excursions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353034306/burgomillodo-dam-and-cantalejo-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgomillodo Dam and Cantalejo Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 32.3 miles (51.9 km) and takes about 2 hours, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069145443/castillos-de-penafiel-y-fuentiduena-por-el-rio-duraton-circular-desde-san-miguel-de-bernuy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Peñafiel y Fuentidueña, por el Río Duratón — circular desde San Miguel de Bernuy&lt;/a&gt; is 55.6 miles (89.5 km) and takes around 3 hours 49 minutes.
tips: If you like river sports and adventure, the small town of San Miguel de Bernuy is your place. A great place to explore the Duraton River by bike and on the water.
This area is a bird lover's paradise in spring after the wet season rains. The Cantalejo Lagoons are home to many migratory and local birds.
Cantalejo, one of the gateways to the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park, is a good place to make a stop during the trip. Have a coffee in Plaza España or eat something small.
Spectacular view of the Burgomillodo Reservoir Dam. A good place to stop for a coffee or something to eat along the way.

The small rural town of Carrascal del Río is one of the gateways to the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park. The most notable monument of the town in addition to the river landscape is the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption. You can rest in the shade of the trees next to the church.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from San Miguel de Bernuy. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and is best suited for cyclists with a good physical condition. While primarily on paved surfaces, there's a possibility of some unpaved or challenging sections, so it's not ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect primarily firm, paved surfaces suitable for road bikes. However, be prepared for potentially varied conditions, as some segments might be unpaved or challenging for bicycles.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular views of the Burgomillodo Dam and winds through the natural environment of the Lagunas de Cantalejo, known for pine forests and wetlands. You'll also pass by the Duratón River in San Miguel de Bernuy and the Plaza de España in Cantalejo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail research suggests that the area around Lagunas de Cantalejo is notable for its unique ecosystem. While not explicitly stated, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential cold/rain in winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required for cycling this specific route. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations before your trip.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for cycling within the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Río Duratón?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Río Duratón. While specific cycling regulations aren't detailed, natural parks often have rules regarding trail use, waste disposal, and respecting wildlife. It's advisable to check the park's official website for any specific guidelines before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is generally not considered suitable or safe for dogs to accompany cyclists, especially given the distance and potential for varied road conditions. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and 51.9 km distance, you should bring a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes), and navigation tools. Sun protection is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both San Miguel de Bernuy and Cantalejo, which the route passes through, offer options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. Cantalejo, in particular, has the Plaza de España, which is often a hub for local amenities.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will cycle along parts of the Senda de la Vega, Calle/Polvorosa Colón, Calle/Polvorosa Huertos, Calle/Polvorosa Empecinado, and Calle del Cubo de Terreña.

waypoints.name: Duratón River in San Miguel de Bernuy
Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Carrascal del Río
